diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Squamous Cell Carcinoma of Esophagus Metastasizing to Psoas Muscle
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The final diagnosis was supported by observations of progressive enlargement of a vaguely hypodense area in the left psoas muscle on follow-up CT scans, which suggested skeletal muscle metastasis. This finding was consistent with the pattern of FDG radioactivity seen in the PET scan, akin to physiological urinary FDG excretion, highlighting the need for contrast-enhanced imaging for accurate diagnosis.
